name: Serrezuela de Bedmar Loop from Jódar
name_original: Serrezuela de Bedmar — circular desde Jódar
description: Challenge yourself on the Serrezuela de Bedmar circular jogging route from Jódar, featuring 9.9 miles and 1869 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging around Jódar offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Mágina Mountains and the Guadalquivir valley. The region is characterized by extensive olive groves and rolling hills, providing numerous scenic routes. The Sierra Mágina Natural Park, which includes part of Jódar's municipal territory, features varied landscapes with mountains and foothills, alongside a rich variety of vegetation. Additionally, the Alto Guadalquivir Natural Space offers flatter paths, contributing to a range of running environments. Best jogging routes around Jódar The most popular jogging route is Las Viñas Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 11 minutes to complete, winding through the region's characteristic olive groves. Another top favourite among local runners is Ascent to Pico de las Cuevas del Aire — circular from Bedmar, a difficult 8.3 miles (13.4 km) path. This route offers challenging uphill sections within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. Local runners also love the Serrezuela de Bedmar Loop from Jódar, a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail leading through varied mountain terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Jogging around Jódar is defined by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and the varied terrain of the Sierra Mágina Mountains. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult running experiences. The routes in Jódar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from a few reviews. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore Jódar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Jódar?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Jódar, offering a variety of experiences for different preferences. These include 11 moderate and 10 difficult trails, primarily within the Sierra Mágina Mountains and surrounding olive groves.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Jódar?
A: The jogging trails in Jódar are characterized by diverse terrain. You'll find routes winding through extensive olive groves and rolling hills in the Guadalquivir valley, as well as more challenging uphill sections within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. The Alto Guadalquivir Natural Space also offers flatter paths near water bodies.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Jódar area?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. For example, the challenging 8.3-mile (13.4 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362871797/ascent-to-pico-de-las-cuevas-del-aire-circular-from-bedmar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pico de las Cuevas del Aire — circular from Bedmar&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning views, and the 9.9-mile (16.0 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362857691/serrezuela-de-bedmar-loop-from-jodar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrezuela de Bedmar Loop from Jódar&lt;/a&gt; takes you through varied mountain terrain.

Q: What are some scenic spots or landmarks I can see while running in Jódar?
A: Jódar's routes offer beautiful scenery. You can explore the Sierra Mágina Natural Park with its diverse vegetation and mountain views. The historic &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/J%C3%B3dar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Jódar&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark, and the charming old town itself provides interesting urban jogging routes. Natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua de Bedmar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Jódar?
A: The running routes in Jódar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from olive groves to mountain paths, and the opportunities for both moderate and challenging experiences.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier jog?
A: While many routes in Jódar are classified as moderate to difficult due to the hilly terrain, the region's extensive olive groves and the flatter paths within the Alto Guadalquivir Natural Space can offer less strenuous options. It's advisable to check the elevation profile of a route before heading out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Jódar?
A: Many natural areas in Spain, including parts of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific trails or parks.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Jódar?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn particularly favorable for outdoor activities like jogging in Jódar. These seasons offer mild temperatures, avoiding the hot, dry summers and the cooler, wetter winters, providing comfortable conditions for your runs.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trail runners, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362863562/serrezuela-de-bedmar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrezuela de Bedmar Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 14 miles (22.7 km) with significant elevation gain, offer a substantial challenge through varied mountain terrain within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by interesting natural features like caves or rivers?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural features. You can find routes that lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the scenic Oleander Route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua de Bedmar&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural highlight in the vicinity that can be explored.

Q: Are there running routes that offer panoramic views of the region?
A: Many routes in Jódar, especially those within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, provide spectacular panoramic views. Trails ascending mountains like Carboneras, La Golondrina, or Jódar itself offer rewarding vistas of the surrounding olive groves and the Guadalquivir valley.
tips: One of the most well-known centers in the south of the town of Úbeda where you can still see the "torreón" that watches over many of its streets.

From afar, you can see this small mountain range that goes from the towns of Bedmar to that of Jódar, offering some rocky mountains between cultivated fields of olive trees that give the impression of cathedrals.

faqs.question: Is the Serrezuela de Bedmar — circular desde Jódar trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as "difficult" and requires a very good physical condition. It spans approximately 16.0 kilometers with 570 meters of elevation gain, featuring significant inclines and challenging terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route features rocky mountains and is mostly composed of paved tracks. However, be prepared for sections with natural scree and exposed slopes, as the wider Serrezuela de Bedmar area is known for its steep, craggy Jurassic limestone formations, ravines, and dolines.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and viewpoints can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular views throughout the route, with rocky mountains set against a backdrop of olive groves. The landscape offers impressive geological formations and vistas of the surrounding mountain ranges. The area around Serrezuela de Bedmar is particularly known for its dramatic rock formations.

faqs.question: Where does the Serrezuela de Bedmar — circular desde Jódar trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Jódar. While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, Jódar is a settlement, so parking options should be available within the town.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Parque Natural de Sierra Mágina. While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash in natural parks to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging terrain. Always check local park regulations before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Given the exposed slopes and challenging nature of the trail, spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit. Temperatures are milder, making the 16.0-kilometer distance and 570 meters of elevation gain more manageable. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder conditions to the mountains.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail or the Parque Natural de Sierra Mágina?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking or jogging on trails within the Parque Natural de Sierra Mágina. However, always respect local regulations and signage, especially concerning protected areas.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Andalucía - North Route (Província de Jaén) and the Plaza del Castillo trail.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for jogging this route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a "great challenge" due to its impressive vertical walls and demanding nature. An insider tip is to be prepared for the significant inclines and potential natural scree. Given the difficulty, starting early in the day is recommended to allow ample time and avoid the midday heat, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: What kind of geological features can I see along the Serrezuela de Bedmar trail?
faqs.answer: The area is renowned for its unique Jurassic limestone geology, which has sculpted a dramatic landscape. You can expect to see steep, craggy formations, ravines, dolines, and potentially natural arches and other impressive rock formations.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Serrezuela de Bedmar — circular desde Jódar jogging route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this 16.0-kilometer route with 570 meters of elevation gain typically takes around 2 hours and 2 minutes (7361 seconds) to complete. This duration assumes a steady jogging pace on difficult terrain.
